message:
🐛 fix(acmm): read blob cache directly in badge fn to fix L2 regression (#9148)

Same-origin HTTP calls from Netlify Functions to the scan endpoint
time out (CDN routing + cold starts exceed the 15s timeout), causing
the badge to fall back to the GitHub tree-scan path which only covers
a subset of criteria — hence the wrong L2 result even after the repo
gained .claude/settings.json and session-summary.md.

Fix: badge function now reads from Netlify Blobs directly (same store
the scan function writes to) before attempting the HTTP call. If the
blob is fresh (< 1h) it uses it immediately; stale or missing falls
through to the HTTP scan as before. force=true bypasses the blob read
and goes straight to a live scan.

Signed-off-by: Andy Anderson <andy@clubanderson.com>
